claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for? If so, could you please tell me their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old, and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's helpful. So you have a son named Alex who is 8 years old.

Now, to determine your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you currently live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's an important part of the eligibility criteria.

Now I need to check a few more things about your circumstances:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have the right to reside in the UK in another way?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I should mention - I don't currently live in the UK. I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's important information.

According to the Child Benefit rules, you may still be able to get Child Benefit if you live abroad in certain circumstances.

**Could you tell me which country you currently live in?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's useful context.

Now, to determine if you can get Child Benefit while living in Spain, I need to understand your residency status:

**Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a Crown servant, or do you have another type of right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any of those. I'm just living in Spain - I don't have settled status or work for the Crown or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. That's important information.

Before I provide my assessment, I need to ask one more thing to be thorough:

**Are you a British citizen?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on the UK government's Child Benefit rules, I'm able to provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary of your eligibility assessment:**

Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, you would **not be eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

**Here's why:**

While you meet many of the basic requirements - you're a British citizen, Alex is 8 years old, and he lives with you - the fundamental criterion for Child Benefit eligibility is that you must **live in the UK**. 

The rules do make some allowance for people living abroad, but only if they:
- Live in certain designated countries, or
- Are Crown servants

Since you're living in Spain and don't fall into either of these categories, you would not currently qualify.

**What you can do:**

I'd recommend contacting HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) directly to confirm this, as circumstances can change and there may be additional information specific to your situation. They'll be able to give you the most up-to-date guidance on whether living abroad affects your eligibility.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about this?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you, which would normally make him eligible for Child Benefit. However, the eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child and you live in the UK. The rules for living abroad specify that you may be able to get Child Benefit if you 'go to live in certain countries' or if you're a Crown servant. While you are a British citizen, this alone does not qualify you for Child Benefit whilst living abroad in Spain. The specific countries where Child Benefit can be claimed abroad, and the conditions for doing so, are detailed in a separate guidance document on the UK government website. Without meeting the specific criteria for living abroad (such as being in one of the designated countries or being a Crown servant), you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex while you are resident in Spain.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are a British citizen with a son, Alex (age 8), who lives with you. However, you currently reside in Spain. While you meet the basic eligibility criteria of being responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, the fundamental requirement to "live in the UK" is not met. The rules do allow for Child Benefit claims in certain circumstances when living abroad, but these require either residence in specific designated countries or Crown service status. As you do not fall into these categories, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. You may wish to contact HMRC directly for the most up-to-date information on which countries qualify for Child Benefit, in case Spain's status has changed."

However, the eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child and you live in the UK. The rules for living abroad specify that you may be able to get Child Benefit if you 'go to live in certain countries' or if you're a Crown servant. While you are a British citizen, this alone does not qualify you for Child Benefit whilst living abroad in Spain. The specific countries where Child Benefit can be claimed abroad, and the conditions for doing so, are detailed in a separate guidance document on the UK government website. Without meeting the specific criteria for living abroad (such as being in one of the designated countries or being a Crown servant), you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex while you are resident in Spain.

While you meet the basic eligibility criteria of being responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, the fundamental requirement to "live in the UK" is not met. The rules do allow for Child Benefit claims in certain circumstances when living abroad, but these require either residence in specific designated countries or Crown service status. As you do not fall into these categories, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. You may wish to contact HMRC directly for the most up-to-date information on which countries qualify for Child Benefit, in case Spain's status has changed."
